name: isGliderDisplayed
label: Show the glider
labelAfter: (if no, glider and its deployments hidden elsewhere except in this page)
title: In rare cases, you might need to hide a glider or its deployments. If you hide a glider or its deployments, they will never appear in the web site except in the registration form where they are visible only by the members of your EGO group. Use the radio buttons for this (default is visible). If you hide a glider, all its deployments will be hidden too. This can be useful for tests.

name: isDeploymentDisplayed
label: Show the deployment
labelAfter: (if no, deployment hidden elsewhere except in this page)
title: In rare cases, you might need to hide a glider or its deployments. If you hide a glider or its deployments, they will never appear in the web site except in the registration form where they are visible only by the members of your EGO group. Use the radio buttons for this (default is visible). If you hide a glider, all its deployments will be hidden too. This can be useful for tests.

name: gliderName
label: Glider Name
title:
EGO json code : platform_code
Definition : Glider’s nick name. The use of lower case is recommended.  This attribute is mandatory.  If you are not using nicknames for gliders, use the serial number instead.
Example :  "platform_code"="pytheas"

name: deploymentName
label: Deployment Name
title:
EGO json code : deployment_code
Definition : Name of the deployment of mission. It is unique among EGO deployments. This code may be used as the local code in catalogues such as SeaDataNet Common Data Index (CDI). If your glider group is not using deployment code, use deployment start date (YYYYMMDD).
Example :  "deployment_code"="Moose T00_19"

name: deploymentLabel
label: Deployment Label
title:
EGO json code : deployment_label
Definition : The deployment label, a free text to describe the deployment.
Example :  “deployment_label”=“Moose T00_19 summer 2010 deployment”

name: WMONumber
label: WMO Number
title:
EGO json code : wmo_platform_code
Definition : WMO (World Meteorological Organization) unique identifier. Any platform of the GOOS has its unique WMO ID. If your glider do not have a WMO ID please contact support@ocean-ops.org.
Example :  "wmo_platform_code"="6100864"
ATTENTION : In the absence of an existing WMO number, put 0 (zero). It will remain editable until an actual number is given by WMO.

name: dataAssemblyCenter
label: Data Assembly Center
title:
EGO json code : data_assembly_center
Definition : Data Assembly Centre (DAC) in charge of this data file. See reference table.
Example :  "data_assembly_center"="IF"

name: summary
label: Summary
title:
EGO json code : summary
Definition : A longer description of the data file. A paragraph of up to 100 words is appropriate. The display of the summary attribute together with the title one should allow data discovery for a human reader.
Example :  “summary”=“Oceanographic glider data from Pytheas glider deployed in gulf of Lion, North-West Mediterranean Sea, in 2019. Measured properties: temperature, salinity, oxygen, turbidity.”

name: principalInvestigator
label: Principal Investigator
title:
EGO json code : principal_investigator
Definition : Name of the principal investigator in charge of the deployment.
Example :  “principal_investigator”=”Pierre Testor”

name: principalInvestigatorEmail
label: PI Email
title:
EGO json code : principal_investigator_email
Definition : Principal investigator’s email address.
Example :  “principal_investigator_email”=“pierre.testor@locean-ipsl.upmc.fr”

name: project
label: Project
title:
EGO json code : project_name
Definition : Name of the project which operates the profiling glider that performed the profile.
Example :  “project_name”=“MOOSE”

name: observatory
label: Observatory
title:
EGO json code : observatory
Definition : Area or line regularly sampled. See reference table.
Example :  “observatory”=“MooseT00”

name: platformFamily
label: Platform Family
title:
EGO json code : PLATFORM_FAMILY
Definition : Autonomous vehicle family
Example :  “PLATFORM_FAMILY”=“Unmanned Underwater Vehicle”

name: platformType
label: Platform Type
title:
EGO json code : PLATFORM_TYPE
Definition :  Type of autonomous vehicle. See reference table.
Example :  “PLATFORM_TYPE”=“Seaglider”

name: gliderSerialNumber
label: Glider Serial nb
title:
EGO json code : GLIDER_SERIAL_NO
Definition : Serial number of the glider.
Example :  "GLIDER_SERIAL_NO"="Unit_196"

name: owningInstitute
label: Owning Institute
title:
EGO json code : GLIDER_OWNER
Definition : The owner of the glider (may be different from the data center and operating institution).
Example :  "GLIDER_OWNER"="LOCEAN"

name: operatingInstitute
label: Operating Institute
title:
EGO json code : OPERATING_INSTITUTION
Definition : The operating institution of the glider (may be different from the glider owner and data center).
Example :  "OPERATING_INSTITUTION”=“DTINSU"

name: deploymenOperator
label: Deployment operator
title:
EGO json code : DEPLOYMENT_OPERATOR
Definition : Name of the person in charge of the glider deployment.
Example :  "Deployment Operator”=“Lou Tine"

name: statusOfDeployment
label: Status of the deployment
title:
For EGO monitoring, it is useful to have the status as declared by the operator. If left empty, the status will be determined according to the (planned)Start and (planned)End Date.
PLANNED: The deployment is planned. Provide the planned start/end dates. The actual start and end dates can be adjusted later.
ACTIVE: The deployment is active. Provide the start date (i.e. when the platform has started to emit).
CLOSED: The deployment is completed.
Cancelled: A planned deployment which was cancelled.

name: startDate
label: (planned) Start Date
title:
EGO json code : DEPLOYMENT_START_DATE
Definition : Date and time (UTC) of deployment of the glider.  Format: YYYYMMDDHHMISS
Example :  “DEPLOYMENT_START_DATE”=“20011230090500” (for December 30th 2001 09:05:00)
COMMENT : If the deployment is only planned, provide the planned start date. When the deployment will start, update.

name: startLatitude
label: (planned) Start Latitude
title:
EGO json code : DEPLOYMENT_START_LATITUDE
Definition : Latitude of the deployment. Unit: decimal degree north. no quotation marks
Example :  “DEPLOYMENT_START_LATITUDE”=44.4991 (for 44° 29’ 56.76’’ N) 
COMMENT : (planned) Start latitude can be approximate. This metadata is used to identify observatories and produce deployment location maps (if no data is shared).

name: startLongitude
label: (planned) Start Longitude
title:
EGO json code : DEPLOYMENT_START_LONGITUDE
Definition : Longitude of the deployment. Unit: decimal degree east. no quotation marks
Example :  “DEPLOYMENT_START_LONGITUDE”=16.7222 (for 16° 43’ 19.92’’ E)
COMMENT : (planned) Start longitude can be approximate. This metadata is used toidentify observatories and produce deployment location maps (if no datais shared).

name: endDate
label: (planned) End Date
title:
EGO json code : DEPLOYMENT_END_DATE
Definition : Date (UTC) of the end of deployment of the glider.
Example :  “DEPLOYMENT_END_DATE”=“20011230090500” (for December 30th 2001 09:05:00)
COMMENT : If the deployment is only planned or operational, provide the planned end date. When the deployment will end, update. This metadata is essential to track the life cycle of the deployment.

name: endLatitude
label: (planned) End Latitude
title:
EGO json code : DEPLOYMENT_END_LATITUDE
Definition : Latitude of the recovery of the glider. Unit: decimal degree north. no quotation marks
Example :  “DEPLOYMENT_END_LATITUDE”=44.4991 (for 44° 29’ 56.76’’ N)

name: endLongitude
label: (planned) End Longitude
title:
EGO json code : DEPLOYMENT_END_LONGITUDE
Definition : Longitude of the recovery of the glider. Unit: degree east. ”” if empty. If a value is present, there should be no quotation marks
Example :  “DEPLOYMENT_END_LONGITUDE”=16.7222 (for 16° 43’ 19.92’’ E)
